commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Telegram chunked reply delivery to suppress native reply metadata for implicit multi-part first-mode replies, fixes regular group thread params, forwards reply policy through outbound sends, and returns multipart receipts for internally chunked text.

PR surface: Source +158, Tests +178. Total +336 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for this PR's narrowed bug boundary: source inspection shows current main can reuse native reply params across direct text chunks, and the PR body reports before/after real Telegram proof with messageUnsupported before and visible BEGIN/MID/END markers after. I did not rerun live Telegram in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A repair worker can fix the narrow lint blocker in the changed test; the native-reply compatibility tradeoff should remain for maintainer review.

target.title: Fix Telegram chunked reply delivery loss
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #96825.

Telegram could visibly lose the first chunk of a long `streaming.mode: "off"` response in group chats. The Bot API accepted the sends, but Telegram Desktop/TDLib rendered the first long formatted native-reply chunk as `messageUnsupported`, so users saw later chunks while the beginning of the answer was hidden.

## Why This Change Was Made

The root cause was treating Telegram native reply/thread metadata as safe decoration for chunked text. For multi-part `replyToMode: "first"` sends, that native reply context can hide text in Telegram clients. The fix centralizes chunk reply policy so multi-part `first` replies consume the reply target but do not attach native reply params. Single-chunk `first` replies still use native reply-to.

The change also fixes the lower-level thread invariant: `scope: "none"` is a regular group reply thread, not a forum/DM topic, so it must not emit `message_thread_id`.

Direct outbound sends now receive the same `replyToMode` policy and return multipart receipts for internally chunked text, so downstream delivery bookkeeping sees all Telegram message ids instead of only the last chunk.

## User Impact

Long Telegram answers should no longer hide the first visible chunk when OpenClaw replies in Telegram groups. Users should see the beginning, middle, and end of chunked final responses instead of seeing only later chunks.

Forum topics and DM topics keep their topic routing. Regular group reply threads no longer leak as Bot API `message_thread_id` params.

## Evidence

- Focused tests:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/channel.message-adapter.test.ts`
  - Result: 5 files passed, 396 tests passed.
- `git diff --check`
- Real Telegram user proof against this branch:
  - Config: `streaming.mode: "off"`, `richMessages: false`, `replyToMode: "first"`.
  - Before fix: first SUT chunk rendered as `messageUnsupported`; `BEGIN` marker missing.
  - After fix: 3 SUT chunks, all `messageText`, `replyToIds: [null, null, null]`, all markers present: `BEGIN`, `MID1`, `MID2`, `END`.
target.author: obviyus
